goal: Land the four warm-tier promotion processors as a tokio-interval-driven `AgentScheduler` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs` — observation @ 60s, convention_signal @ 60s, architecture_delta @ 120s, decision_linker @ 60s — each promoting unpromoted hot-tier rows to the corresponding warm-tier table on schedule. Mirrors the SEXTUPLE-validated G-backbone DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam (WO-0047 G1, WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7, WO-0089 G8) — defines `WarmProcessorSource` trait + 4 concrete `WarmProcessorKind` variants + orchestration shell, leaving production wiring (real KG handle adapter) for a follow-up WO. Single-feature WO; no peer features in feature-list share the warm-tier-promotion thematic invariant — F11/F14/F16 are integration-test suites that depend on F13 transitively.
plan_summary: Create NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs` (~700-900 LOC including frozen test) with a `WarmProcessorSource` trait (DEC-0008 §4 dep-inversion seam: production impl owns live `KnowledgeGraph` handle; test impl is in-memory) + a `WarmProcessorKind` enum naming the four processors + an `AgentScheduler` struct that drives each processor on a per-kind tokio interval (60s, 60s, 120s, 60s per master-plan §10 [knowledge_tiering] config block lines 2015-2024). Each processor: (a) selects unpromoted rows from the relevant `hot_*` table via the trait, (b) applies the rule-based promotion criterion (observation: dedup by `related_symbol` similarity ≥ 0.9; convention_signal: pattern_hash count ≥ 3 — the `convention_min_evidence` config; architecture_delta: at least one unpromoted delta per change_type+file_path → aggregate into warm_architecture_state; decision_linker: hot_decision_material rows with non-null affected_files), (c) inserts into the corresponding warm_* table, (d) marks hot rows as promoted=1. Frozen test `pub async fn test_warm_processors` lives at module root, drives the scheduler with `tokio::time::pause` + `advance` against a `TestWarmProcessorSource` with seeded hot rows, asserts each processor fires at its scheduled interval and promotes the expected rows. Pre-baked M1/M2/M3 mutation contract in the RFR. `lib.rs` declares `pub mod agent_scheduler;` alphabetically (between `:` and the existing `branch_manager;` — wait, alphabetical: `agent_scheduler;` precedes `branch_manager;`).
scope_in: 1. **Single-feature warm-tier-promotion WO**: P3-W10-F13 has no peer features in the feature-list sharing the warm-tier-promotion thematic invariant. F11/F14/F16 are integration test suites that depend on F13 transitively (cannot bundle pre-F13-landing). The G-backbone template is SEXTUPLE-validated (WO-0047 G1, WO-0070 G3, WO-0083 G4, WO-0085 G7, WO-0089 G8); single-feature WOs are precedent-supported (WO-0079/WO-0080/WO-0087/WO-0089/WO-0091/WO-0092 single-feature) AND substantial enough to warrant a dedicated WO (4 processors × ~80-150 LOC each + orchestration shell + frozen test = ~700-900 LOC).

2. **F13 module file location**: NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs` (file does NOT exist at planner-time — pre-flight `! test -e crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs` returns true). This is a layering reinterpretation of master-plan §17.2 line 1636 which lists `warm_processors.rs` under `ucil-core/src/`: the feature acceptance test selector `-p ucil-daemon agent_scheduler::test_warm_processors` (per feature-list.json:P3-W10-F13.acceptance_tests[0].selector) pins both the crate (`ucil-daemon`) AND the module name (`agent_scheduler`). Per DEC-0008 §4 (UCIL-owned trait dep-inversion seam) precedent — set by G3/G4/G7/G8 placing trait+orchestration in the daemon (where the live KG handle lives) — the warm-processor orchestration belongs in `ucil-daemon` despite §17.2's earlier suggestion. Mirror the existing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/g8.rs` shape verbatim — same module-header crate-doc style, same constant-naming convention, same `Send + Sync` trait bounds.

3. **F13 frozen-test placement**: at MODULE ROOT (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0068 lessons §'For planner: module-root placement is REQUIRED for substring-match selector resolution'. The selector `-p ucil-daemon agent_scheduler::test_warm_processors` per feature-list.json:P3-W10-F13.acceptance_tests[0].selector pins both module name (`agent_scheduler`) AND function name. Test fn signature: `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread", start_paused = true)] pub async fn test_warm_processors() { ... }`. The `start_paused = true` flag enables deterministic `tokio::time::advance(Duration::from_secs(60))` driving — same shape as `cross_group::CROSS_GROUP_*` test patterns from WO-0068. The `TestWarmProcessorSource` impl lives in the same file under `#[cfg(test)]` adjacent to (or as nested helpers within) the test fn — same shape as `TestG3Source`/`TestG4Source`/`TestG7Source`/`TestG8Source` from WO-0070/WO-0083/WO-0085/WO-0089.

4. **Module constants** (with full rustdoc citing master-plan §10 [knowledge_tiering] config block lines 2015-2024 + a `# Examples` block where non-trivial per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    `pub const OBSERVATION_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(60);` per master-plan §10 line 2016 `observation_processor_interval_sec = 60`.
    `pub const CONVENTION_SIGNAL_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(60);` per master-plan §10 line 2017 `convention_signal_processor_interval_sec = 60`.
    `pub const ARCHITECTURE_DELTA_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(120);` per master-plan §10 line 2018 `architecture_delta_processor_interval_sec = 120`.
    `pub const DECISION_LINKER_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(60);` per master-plan §10 line 2019 `decision_linker_interval_sec = 60`.
    `pub const CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE: usize = 3;` per master-plan §10 line 2020 `convention_min_evidence = 3`.
    `pub const OBSERVATION_DEDUP_THRESHOLD: f64 = 0.9;` per master-plan §10 line 2024 `observation_dedup_threshold = 0.9`.
    Use `tokio::time::*` only — never `std::time::*` for timing in async paths per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Async. All 6 constants documented inline citing the §10 line where the value is set.

5. **Public types** (each with full rustdoc + `# Examples` block where non-trivial):
    (a) `pub enum WarmProcessorKind { Observation, ConventionSignal, ArchitectureDelta, DecisionLinker }` — four variants matching the four processors per master-plan §10 lines 2016-2019 verbatim. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. The `Hash + Eq` bounds are required for HashMap/HashSet use in the orchestrator's per-kind interval registry. Each variant's rustdoc cites the master-plan line where the kind is named.
    (b) `pub struct WarmPromotionResult { pub kind: WarmProcessorKind, pub hot_rows_examined: u64, pub warm_rows_inserted: u64, pub hot_rows_marked_promoted: u64, pub error: Option<String> }`. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. Per-tick result returned by each processor. Invariant: `warm_rows_inserted + dropped_due_to_threshold == hot_rows_examined` (rustdoc-asserted; not enforced at type level).
    (c) `pub struct AgentSchedulerStats { pub ticks_observed: BTreeMap<WarmProcessorKind, u64>, pub last_result: BTreeMap<WarmProcessorKind, WarmPromotionResult> }`. `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]`. Aggregated scheduler stats — counter per kind + last per-kind result. Use `BTreeMap` (not `HashMap`) for deterministic iteration order in tests.
    (d) `pub struct AgentSchedulerHandle { /* opaque: cancellation token + JoinSet */ }`. Returned by `AgentScheduler::start(...)`; calling `.shutdown().await` drains all four processor tasks gracefully via `tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken`. NO `#[non_exhaustive]` — stable contract.
    Each struct MUST derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq` minimum; `Serialize, Deserialize` for the wire-shape types per WO-0085 §planner precedent.

6. **`WarmProcessorSource` trait** — DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam: `pub trait WarmProcessorSource: Send + Sync + 'static { ... }` with these methods (each `async`, returning `Result<T, WarmProcessorError>` for an internal `WarmProcessorError` enum derived via `thiserror`):
    (a) `async fn select_unpromoted_observations(&self, limit: usize) -> Result<Vec<HotObservationRow>, WarmProcessorError>;` — production impl runs `SELECT id, raw_text, session_id, related_file, related_symbol, created_at FROM hot_observations WHERE promoted_to_warm = 0 ORDER BY created_at LIMIT ?` against the live KG.
    (b) `async fn insert_warm_observation(&self, row: WarmObservationRow) -> Result<i64, WarmProcessorError>;` — `INSERT INTO warm_observations` with the deduplicated text + aggregated evidence_count.
    (c) `async fn mark_observations_promoted(&self, hot_ids: &[i64]) -> Result<(), WarmProcessorError>;` — `UPDATE hot_observations SET promoted_to_warm = 1 WHERE id IN (?)`.
    Repeat the (select-unpromoted, insert-warm, mark-promoted) triplet for the other three kinds: `select_unpromoted_convention_signals` / `insert_warm_convention` / `mark_convention_signals_promoted`; `select_unpromoted_architecture_deltas` / `upsert_warm_architecture_state` / `mark_architecture_deltas_promoted`; `select_unpromoted_decision_material` / `insert_warm_decision` / `mark_decision_material_promoted`. 12 trait methods total. The trait is `Send + Sync + 'static` so the scheduler can `Arc<dyn WarmProcessorSource>` it across processor tasks.

7. **Internal POD row types** (NOT pub re-exports of `KnowledgeGraph` row decoders — this is the dep-inversion seam): `pub struct HotObservationRow { pub id: i64, pub raw_text: String, pub session_id: Option<String>, pub related_file: Option<String>, pub related_symbol: Option<String>, pub created_at: String }`. Similar `HotConventionSignalRow`, `HotArchitectureDeltaRow`, `HotDecisionMaterialRow`, `WarmObservationRow`, `WarmConventionRow`, `WarmArchitectureStateRow`, `WarmDecisionRow`. All `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]`. Field names match the SQL schema (master-plan §11 lines 1213-1320 verbatim) so the production adapter is a 1:1 rusqlite pluck. NO `Serialize/Deserialize` on these (internal-only — not on the wire).

8. **Per-kind processor functions**: `pub async fn run_observation_processor<S: WarmProcessorSource>(source: &S) -> Result<WarmPromotionResult, WarmProcessorError>`. Selects ≤256 unpromoted hot_observations per tick (constant `WARM_PROCESSOR_BATCH_SIZE: usize = 256` documented inline), groups by `related_symbol` (using the `OBSERVATION_DEDUP_THRESHOLD = 0.9` Jaro-Winkler similarity on `raw_text` for groups with the same `related_symbol`; if `related_symbol` is None, dedup by `raw_text` similarity alone), inserts one warm_observations row per cluster with `evidence_count = cluster.len() as i64`, marks all source hot rows as promoted. Repeat with adapted shape for the other 3 kinds (convention_signal: count by pattern_hash, promote when ≥ CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE = 3; architecture_delta: aggregate by (change_type, file_path) into warm_architecture_state.summary; decision_linker: filter to rows with non-null affected_files, insert one warm_decisions per row with title = first 80 chars of description). Each function is `async` so `tokio::time::timeout` wraps each source-method call with `WARM_PROCESSOR_OP_DEADLINE = Duration::from_secs(30)`.

9. **`AgentScheduler` struct** with public methods: `pub fn new(source: Arc<dyn WarmProcessorSource>) -> Self`; `pub async fn start(&self) -> AgentSchedulerHandle` — spawns 4 tokio tasks via `tokio::task::JoinSet`, each running `loop { tokio::select! { _ = cancel.cancelled() => break, _ = interval.tick() => { let result = run_<kind>_processor(&source).await; stats.write().last_result.insert(kind, result); stats.write().ticks_observed.entry(kind).and_modify(|c| *c += 1).or_insert(1); } } }`; `pub fn stats(&self) -> AgentSchedulerStats` — snapshot read of the shared `Arc<RwLock<AgentSchedulerStats>>`. The `AgentSchedulerHandle::shutdown(self) -> Result<(), WarmProcessorError>` drains the JoinSet via cancel-token + await all tasks. No `JoinHandle` leaks.

10. **§15.2 tracing on the orchestrator**: `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.agent.warm_processor", skip(source), fields(kind = ?kind))]` on each `run_<kind>_processor` function. The orchestration loop wraps each tick in `tracing::info_span!("ucil.agent.warm_processor.tick", kind = ?kind)`. Per master-plan §15.2 lines 1518-1522 + WO-0070/WO-0085 precedent.

11. **`WarmProcessorError`** enum derived via `thiserror::Error` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors (libraries: thiserror; never `.unwrap`/`.expect` outside `#[cfg(test)]`). Variants: `Source(String)`, `Timeout`, `Database(String)`, `Cancelled`. `#[non_exhaustive]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors.

12. **`WARM_PROCESSOR_OP_DEADLINE` deadline as UNCONDITIONAL `const` (30 s)**, NOT min'd with caller-supplied deadlines. Same shape as WO-0068's `cross_group::CROSS_GROUP_PER_GROUP_DEADLINE`, WO-0070's `g3::G3_PER_SOURCE_DEADLINE`. Avoids the deadline-collapse race where `tokio::time::Timeout::poll` (polls inner-first) lets per-source win on tight masters. WO-0068 lessons §'For executor: per-group deadline MUST be strictly larger than tight master deadlines' explicitly applies.

13. **Module-coherence per DEC-0005**: bundle the NEW `agent_scheduler.rs` source file + `crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml` deps + `lib.rs` `pub mod agent_scheduler;` declaration + `lib.rs` `pub use agent_scheduler::{...}` re-export block in ONE cohesive feat commit, even if it crosses 700-900 LOC. Splitting helper-by-helper produces stub-shaped intermediate states the critic flags AND breaks the M1/M2/M3 mutation-restoration contract (all three mutations target sites inside the same file). Cite DEC-0005 + WO-0068 cross_group.rs (788 LOC) + WO-0067 ceqp.rs (548 LOC) + WO-0089 g8.rs (~700 LOC) precedent in the commit body.

14. **`lib.rs` module declaration alphabetical placement**: insert `pub mod agent_scheduler;` BEFORE `pub mod branch_manager;` (alphabetical: `agent_scheduler` < `branch_manager`). The existing `pub mod` block at `lib.rs:156-175` is alphabetical-sorted. Re-export block: `pub use agent_scheduler::{AgentScheduler, AgentSchedulerHandle, AgentSchedulerStats, WarmProcessorKind, WarmProcessorSource, WarmPromotionResult, WarmProcessorError, OBSERVATION_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL, CONVENTION_SIGNAL_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL, ARCHITECTURE_DELTA_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL, DECISION_LINKER_INTERVAL, CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE, OBSERVATION_DEDUP_THRESHOLD};` placed alphabetically — BEFORE the existing `pub use branch_manager::{...}` block at `lib.rs:187`. WO-0068 lesson §'pick ONE — alphabetical OR positional, not both' resolved here as 'alphabetical' for both `pub mod` and `pub use`.

15. **Cargo.toml deps**: add `tokio-util = { version = "0.7", features = ["sync", "rt"] }` workspace dep if not already present (for `CancellationToken`). Use `=` with at least one space before the `=` to satisfy AC pattern `^\+[a-z_-]+\s*=`. WO-0068 lesson §'Async-trait workspace dep added with .workspace = true (no whitespace before =)' carried — use `tokio-util.workspace = true` if added at workspace level, otherwise direct dep. The `strsim` crate (for Jaro-Winkler similarity) MAY be added if not present; alternatively, hand-roll a 30-LOC token-overlap similarity to avoid a new dep — prefer the latter to keep `ucil-daemon` lean. Document the choice in the commit body.

16. **Frozen test contract** — `pub async fn test_warm_processors` covers SA1..SA8:
    SA1: observation processor fires at 60s tick; promotes 1 warm row from 1 hot observation; `mark_promoted` called with [hot_id].
    SA2: convention_signal processor: 3 hot rows with same pattern_hash → 1 warm row inserted (evidence_count = 3); 2 hot rows with different pattern_hash → 0 warm rows (below CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE = 3).
    SA3: architecture_delta processor fires at 120s tick (NOT 60s); 2 hot deltas with same (change_type, file_path) → 1 upsert into warm_architecture_state with summary mentioning both deltas.
    SA4: decision_linker processor at 60s tick; 1 hot decision_material with affected_files=`["src/main.rs"]` → 1 warm_decisions inserted; 1 hot row with affected_files=`null` → 0 warm rows.
    SA5: at t=120s, ALL FOUR processors have fired (observation: 2 ticks, convention: 2 ticks, architecture: 1 tick, decision: 2 ticks); `stats.ticks_observed` matches.
    SA6: per-tick error in one processor (mocked via TestWarmProcessorSource error injection) does NOT kill the scheduler; subsequent ticks fire normally.
    SA7: tracing spans `ucil.agent.warm_processor.tick` emitted (capture via `tracing-subscriber` test layer; assert ≥4 spans by t=120s).
    SA8: `AgentSchedulerHandle::shutdown().await` returns `Ok(())` AND all 4 inner tasks have completed (verified via `JoinSet::is_empty()`).
    Each SA carries `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` panic message body per DEC-0007 + WO-0067/68/69/70 precedent.

17. **M1/M2/M3 mutation contract** — pre-baked in the RFR with file paths, exact patches, expected SA failures, and restore commands:
    M1: change `OBSERVATION_PROCESSOR_INTERVAL: Duration = Duration::from_secs(60)` → `Duration::from_secs(7200)` (2-hour interval). Expected: SA1 fails (0 ticks observed at t=120s) AND SA5 fails (`stats.ticks_observed[Observation] != 2`). Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs`.
    M2: in `run_convention_signal_processor`, change `if pattern_hash_count >= CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE` → `if pattern_hash_count >= CONVENTION_MIN_EVIDENCE + 5`. Expected: SA2 fails (0 warm rows inserted from 3 hot rows; but SA2 expected 1). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    M3: in `run_architecture_delta_processor`, replace the aggregator's group-by `(change_type, file_path)` with group-by `change_type` only. Expected: SA3 fails (warm summary becomes ambiguous across files; assertion on file_path-specific summary text fails). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    Each mutation patch is verbatim-quoted in the RFR's 'Mutation contract' section. Verifier applies via `Edit` (in-place, no `git stash`), takes md5 snapshot before any mutation, restores via `git checkout --`, confirms md5 match. WO-0067/WO-0068/WO-0069/WO-0070 precedent.

18. **`#[allow(dead_code)]` on private helpers if M1 mutation orphans them**: if any private helper becomes orphaned under M1 (interval bumped to 7200s → processor never fires → helper never called), pre-emptively annotate with `#[allow(dead_code)]` + a rustdoc comment citing the M1 mutation contract. WO-0070 lessons §'Atomic M1 mutation contract via #[allow(dead_code)] on private helper' explicitly applies. Without this, `#![deny(warnings)]` (lib.rs) converts the dead-code warning into a compile error and the verifier observes a build failure instead of the SA1 panic.

19. **Word-ban scrub coverage**: pre-flight grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs scripts/verify/P3-W10-F13.sh` MUST return empty for production-side files. Test-side `TestWarmProcessorSource` impl + tracing-subscriber test layer `Visit` collector live under `#[cfg(test)]` and are exempt under WO-0048 line 363's #[cfg(test)] carve-out + WO-0068 lessons §'Test helpers under #[cfg(test)] exempt'. WO-0068's separate `docs(daemon): scrub fakes from agent_scheduler production-code comment` follow-up commit pattern is the safety-net if a stray `// fake` slips into a non-`#[cfg(test)]` comment.

20. **`scripts/verify/P3-W10-F13.sh` shape**: shebang `#!/usr/bin/env bash`, `set -euo pipefail`, run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon agent_scheduler::test_warm_processors --no-fail-fast 2>&1 | tee /tmp/p3w10f13.log` and assert via `grep -qE 'test result: ok\. 1 passed' /tmp/p3w10f13.log`. Mirror `scripts/verify/P3-W11-F09.sh` (WO-0089) verbatim shape — same `--list` count check + `test result: ok` grep. Add a sanity grep `grep -qE 'pub mod agent_scheduler;' crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`. Exit 0 on green, exit 1 otherwise. Mark `chmod +x` in the same commit.

21. **`#![deny(warnings)]` interaction with `#[allow(dead_code)]`**: scope_in #18's allow-dead-code for M1-orphaned helpers is the ONLY dead-code allowance; ALL other unused-code surfaces MUST be either deleted or actually used. Critic check 1 (Warnings) examines this: any other `#[allow(dead_code)]` without a M1-mutation-citation rustdoc is a soft warning.

22. **No `std::process::Command` in production paths** per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Async (`tokio::process::Command` only). The agent_scheduler does NOT spawn subprocesses (it talks to the KG via the trait), so this is preventive only — but the AC grep (`! grep -qE 'std::process::Command'`) is the standing safety-net.
23. **No `unsafe` blocks** in `agent_scheduler.rs` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Unsafe (forbidden by default; ADR required). The AC grep `! grep -qE 'unsafe\s*\{|unsafe\s+fn'` enforces.

24. **No `tokio::test` without `flavor = "current_thread"` + `start_paused = true`** for the frozen test — required for deterministic `tokio::time::advance` driving. Single-thread current-thread runtime + paused clock is the canonical pattern for interval-driven scheduler tests. AC grep: `grep -qE 'tokio::test\(.*start_paused\s*=\s*true' crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler.rs`.

25. **Forbidden-pattern declaration in the rustdoc header** of `agent_scheduler.rs`: explicit module-level rustdoc paragraph stating 'No mocks of SQLite / KnowledgeGraph / tokio::process::Command in production paths; the `WarmProcessorSource` trait is the dep-inversion seam (DEC-0008 §4) — production impls MUST own a real `KnowledgeGraph` handle.' Critic check 2 (Mocking forbidden) verifies this exists.

26. **No new dependency without ADR**: prefer hand-rolled token-overlap similarity (~30 LOC) over adding `strsim` crate dep. If `strsim` IS added, write a brief inline justification in the Cargo.toml comment. Same shape as WO-0067's `extract_gaps` byte-scan in lieu of `regex` dep — keep `ucil-daemon` lean per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate layout.

27. **Phase-1/Phase-2/Phase-3 gate sub-checks expected disclosed deviations**: AC36/AC37 (phase-1, phase-2 gate runs) are routinely re-run by the verifier from a clean session per WO-0067/WO-0068/WO-0069 precedent. The `effectiveness-gate.sh` step spawns a long-running `claude -p` sub-session that frequently exceeds the executor's session window. Executor runs constituent sub-checks (cargo-test + clippy + MCP-smoke + Serena-docker + diagnostics-bridge + real-repo-smoke + multi-lang-probes + ucil-core/treesitter/lsp-diagnostics coverage gates) in isolation; verifier re-runs the gate-script entry point from a clean session. Three pre-existing flake escalations carry as standing scope_out: `20260507T0357Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-rs-line-flake.md`, `20260507T1629Z-effectiveness-refactor-rename-python-fixture-missing-symbol.md`, `20260507T1930Z-effectiveness-nav-rust-symbol-doctest-caller-flake.md`.

28. **Standing coverage workaround now 26+ WOs deep**: continue citing scope_out + AC23 standing protocol until a dedicated harness-improvement WO replaces `coverage-gate.sh` to use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER`. Use `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-daemon --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` for substantive coverage check (floor 80% per WO-0069 baseline). Skip the `[FAIL] coverage gate: ucil-{core,daemon,embeddings}` lines per scope_out + AC23 standing protocol.

29. **`ucil-build/verification-reports/**` AND `ucil-build/escalations/**` AND `ucil-build/critic-reports/**` carved out from forbidden_paths**: the phase-3 effectiveness-evaluator + gate-check child agents commit refresh artefacts and `harness-config` escalations to the feat branch during gate execution. WO-0067/WO-0068/WO-0069 scope_in precedent.

30. **`scripts/reality-check.sh` known-bug carve-out**: its unconditional `git stash pop` interacts badly with pre-existing unrelated stashes from earlier session runs. Symptom: false-positive merge conflicts in unrelated `verification-reports/coverage-*.md` files. Workaround: the M1/M2/M3 in-place mutation contract from the RFR is the authoritative anti-laziness layer — reality-check.sh is informational only. Bucket B candidate for triage. WO-0067/WO-0068/WO-0069 precedent.

31. **Conventional Commits subject-line target ≤70 chars** per .claude/rules/commit-style.md (soft target). WO-0069 had 3/7 subjects exceeding 70 chars and critic flagged as advisory only. For WO-0093, prefer 'feat(daemon): land warm-tier promotion processors via AgentScheduler' (62 chars) over longer alternatives.

32. **Bundle commit body MUST cite**: 'Phase: 3', 'Feature: P3-W10-F13', 'Work-order: WO-0093' trailers per .claude/rules/commit-style.md. Co-Authored-By trailer per the rule. Body body MUST cite DEC-0005 (module-coherence) + DEC-0008 §4 (UCIL-owned trait dep-inversion seam) + WO-0070 / WO-0089 precedent for the scheduler-orchestration pattern.
scope_out: 1. NO production wiring of a real-`KnowledgeGraph` `WarmProcessorSource` impl. That's a follow-up WO that lands `crates/ucil-daemon/src/agent_scheduler/kg_source.rs` (or similar) wiring `Arc<RwLock<KnowledgeGraph>>` into the trait — paired with the `lifecycle.rs` startup wiring of `AgentScheduler::start()`. Mirrors the deferred G3/G4/G7/G8 production-source wiring pattern.

2. NO daemon `lifecycle.rs` startup wiring — the scheduler is created and started by the lifecycle bootstrap in a follow-up WO. WO-0093 ships only the trait + 4 processors + orchestration + frozen test (with TestWarmProcessorSource).
3. NO MCP tool exposure of warm-tier results. Warm-tier rows are consumed by `tier_merger` (already passing in P3-W10-F12); no new MCP surface.

4. NO modification of `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` — the trait talks to the KG via the dep-inversion seam, not via direct edits to KG. If a new KG public method is needed (e.g., `select_unpromoted_observations`), that's a follow-up WO; WO-0093 expects all required reads/writes to compose from the existing KG public surface.

5. NO `crates/ucil-core/src/warm_processors.rs` — the master-plan §17.2 line 1636 layering is reinterpreted per DEC-0008 §4 (trait+orchestration in daemon). Future cross-cutting refactor MAY relocate the trait to `ucil-core` (if a non-daemon consumer emerges); WO-0093 keeps it in `ucil-daemon`.
6. NO benchmarks / criterion suite — feature description does not require performance bounds (intervals are 60s/120s; per-tick wall is dominated by SQLite). Bench follow-up if AC fails.
7. NO standing coverage-gate workaround removal — covered by separate Bucket B / Bucket D triage WO when prioritised.
8. NO `effectiveness-gate.sh` flake remediation — covered by separate Bucket B harness-improvement WO when prioritised.

9. NO P3-W11-F12 (feedback loop) bundling — different crate (ucil-core::feedback), different concern (post-hoc analysis of bonus_context category usage), no shared master-plan-§N.M contract beyond the loose 'agent automation' theme. Single-feature WO discipline preserved per WO-0089/WO-0091/WO-0092 precedent.

precedent:WO-0089 G8 backbone — single-feature WO; ~700 LOC g8.rs new file; WarmProcessorSource analogue G8Source trait; module-root frozen test; M1/M2/M3 mutation contract template
precedent:WO-0085 G7 backbone — 3-feature bundle template; M1+M2+M3 mutation contract; module-root frozen tests; pure-deterministic tracing carve-out
precedent:WO-0083 G4 backbone — TestG4Source #[cfg(test)] impl pattern; tokio::test(flavor=multi_thread); SA-tagged panic bodies
precedent:WO-0070 G3 backbone — tokio interval-driven module; module-root test placement; spirit-over-literal mutation carve-out; #[allow(dead_code)] on M1-orphaned helpers
precedent:WO-0068 cross_group — per-source deadline UNCONDITIONAL const NOT min'd with master_deadline; alphabetical lib.rs placement preferred over positional
lessons_applied: WO-0067 §planner: pre-bake M1/M2/M3 mutation contracts in scope_in[].acceptance with file/patch/SA/restore quadruplets — verifier should not have to invent the mutation patch.

WO-0068 §planner: module-root frozen test placement is REQUIRED for substring-match selector resolution (not under `mod tests {...}`); pre-emptively prescribe `#[cfg(test)] pub async fn` at module root.
WO-0068 §planner: per-source-deadline UNCONDITIONAL `const` (NOT min'd with caller-supplied master); avoids `tokio::time::Timeout::poll` inner-first race.

WO-0068 §planner: lib.rs re-export block — pick ONE rule (alphabetical OR positional); WO-0093 picks alphabetical for both `pub mod` and `pub use` per the established lib.rs:156-175 alphabetical-sorted pattern.
WO-0069 §planner: pre-emptively allow API-key-style env-gate short-circuits if CRUD smoke needs credentials — N/A for WO-0093 (no external API; KG-only).
WO-0069 §planner: AC36/AC37 phase-N gate runs disclosed-deviated by verifier per standing flake escalations — scope_out documented.
WO-0070 §planner: pre-emptively annotate `#[allow(dead_code)]` on private helpers that the M1 mutation will orphan — required by `#![deny(warnings)]` to keep mutation atomic.

WO-0089 §planner: single-feature G-backbone WOs are precedent-supported when no peer features share the thematic invariant; ~700 LOC scope is appropriate for the trait+orchestration+frozen-test bundle.
WO-0085 §planner: pure-deterministic-fallback note exemption from §15.2 tracing — N/A here; warm processors ARE async/IO orchestration so tracing applies.
Carry: standing coverage-gate sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround (`env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov ...`); 26+ WOs deep — Bucket B candidate.
